title: Target-Aggressor Attack Resolution Software
content:
{{Top|leg}}
The '''Target-Aggressor Attack Resolution Software (TAARS)''' was a type of [[Computer/Legends|computer]] program that helped [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to locate and identify their targets. As a prank, members of the reconstructed [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] reprogrammed the TAARS systems of the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Horton Salm/Legends|Horton Salm's]] [[Defender Wing]] to produce the Rogue Squadron crest on the fighters' primary monitors when one of them was "shot down" during a training mission.

This tampering greatly upset the general; however, it could be said that he was more infuriated by the exercise's results, which proved embarrassing to him as the Rogues had decimated his unit with minimal loss of their own.

==Appearances==
*''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Rogue Squadron (unabridged audiobook)}}

==Sources==
*<!-- 2008-12-09 -->''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''
